thread_tweets.text:
Just for clarification: 

-> Thuringia’s (central germany) Prime Minister Mario Voigt and Economy Minister Colette Boos-John made the announcement in Erfurt today.

-> EU CHIPS ACT level technical approval was end of last year, but German federal and state governments today formalized and legally deployed the capital

-> Today's press conference released the finalized financial structure

which is why new reporting was today.

I was debating the evolution of art, and entertainment with someone who had the stance that  everything in culture accelerates and becomes better because it’s able use the lessons of the past to improve.

This is true for most movies prior to 1990s. They can be unwatchable to modern audiences. Same with TV shows. Definitely with a lot of sports…if you watched a tennis match from the 70’s you’d be bored.

Novels seem to escape this. Paintings, art, buildings as well. Some things we can build upon, others not so much. Perfected forms. 

Makes me wonder whether we can push the boundaries here with AI. Will we accelerate yet again? Will our future generations only want AI content because it will be so good?

tweets.text:
ETH gets a lot of flack for underperforming, everyone tries to blame something or someone (leadership)

But maybe it just ran too fast before, maybe it was never really so valuable to justify its 2021 valuation - maybe market got it wrong back then and spent last 5 years self correcting

Its nearest competitor is SOL, it has it beat in some KPIs and yet its still 5 times cheaper, and some people will claim even SOL as expensive

In the short run markets are voting machine, in the long run they are weighting machines

Maybe we are just coming from voting phase to weighting phase and things are as they should be
